message:
Add configuration management scaffolding

Establish config module structure with placeholder AppConfig type for
future TOML-based application configuration.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 3.1, § 11):
The config layer manages persistent user preferences and system settings
via platform-appropriate configuration files (XDG Base Directory spec
on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, %APPDATA% on Windows)
using the directories crate for path resolution.

Planned mod.rs responsibilities:
- Configuration file location: Use directories::ProjectDirs::from("com",
  "coding-agent-search", "coding-agent-search") to compute config
  directory (e.g., ~/.config/coding-agent-search/ on Linux), create
  directory if missing (fs::create_dir_all), read config.toml if present
  or initialize with defaults and write to disk for user editing.

- TOML schema definition: Define AppConfig struct with serde Deserialize
  trait matching PLAN § 11.1 example config.toml structure:

  [general] section:
  - theme: String = "dark" | "light" for UI color scheme selection
  - enable_tantivy: bool = true to use Tantivy index for search, false
    to fall back to SQLite FTS5 (useful for debugging or when Tantivy
    index corrupted)
  - max_results: usize = 200 for top-K result limiting in UI

  [sqlite] section:
  - path: PathBuf with default ~/.local/share/coding-agent-search/
    agent_search.db, user can override for custom database location
  - page_size: u32 = 4096 for SQLite page size (trade-off between I/O
    efficiency and memory usage, 4KB is common default)
  - cache_size_mb: u32 = 64 for PRAGMA cache_size computation (64MB
    page cache provides good hit rate for typical corpus size)

  [agents.<slug>] sections for each of the six agents:
  - [agents.codex]: enabled: bool = true, home: PathBuf with default
    ~/.codex or $CODEX_HOME env override detection
  - [agents.claude_code]: enabled: bool, projects_dir: PathBuf =
    ~/.claude/projects
  - [agents.gemini_cli]: enabled: bool, root: PathBuf = ~/.gemini/tmp
  - [agents.amp]: enabled: bool = false (default disabled given limited
    local cache per PLAN § 2.4), note: String documenting limitation
  - [agents.cline]: enabled: bool, vscode_profile: String = "Code" vs
    "Code - Insiders" for globalStorage path disambiguation
  - [agents.opencode]: enabled: bool, search_project_roots: bool = true
    to scan upward from CWD for .opencode directories, extra_db_paths:
    Vec<PathBuf> for explicit SQLite database locations

  [search] section (advanced tuning per PLAN § 11.2):
  - default_time_range: String = "7d" for initial UI time filter
  - min_query_length: usize = 1 minimum characters to trigger search

  [tantivy] section:
  - index_path: PathBuf for custom index location override
  - num_indexing_threads: usize = 1 (Tantivy writer thread count)

- Configuration merging: Layer config sources in precedence order:
  (1) Hardcoded defaults struct, (2) config.toml if exists, (3) env
  var overrides (e.g., CODEX_HOME overrides agents.codex.home). Use
  toml::from_str() to parse TOML into AppConfig, anyhow::Context for
  helpful error messages on parse failures.

- Auto-detection fallback: If config.toml missing on first run, invoke
  agent detection heuristics (check for executables in PATH via which
  crate per PLAN § 10.1, probe filesystem for signature files per
  PLAN § 10.2) and populate [agents.<slug>].enabled and paths with
  detected values. Write generated config.toml to disk with helpful
  comments (using toml crate's manual Value construction + pretty
  serialization for comment preservation, since serde_derive loses
  comments).

- Runtime reload support: Implement config file watcher (via notify
  crate already in Cargo.toml) to detect config.toml modifications,
  reload and broadcast ConfigUpdate message to UI thread for dynamic
  theme changes, agent enable/disable toggling, etc. without restart.
  Per PLAN § 13.2 v1 milestone: "dynamic reload (r to reload config)".

Integration with dotenvy:
Config module does NOT read .env file directly—main.rs already called
dotenvy::dotenv().ok() before run() per AGENTS.md requirement. Config
module uses env::var() for overrides (e.g., CODEX_HOME) which will
already have .env variables loaded. This separation keeps config layer
env-agnostic (works identically in test harness where .env may not
exist).

Default config strategy:
Prefer "discover and enable" over "prompt user"—if a supported agent is
detected (binary in PATH or data directory exists), auto-enable it in
default config. User can disable via config.toml editing or future TUI
settings screen (PLAN § 10.3 detection UI). This reduces first-run
friction: just run agent-search and it works if you have Codex/Claude/etc
already installed, no configuration required.

This commit contains only the module declaration (mod.rs) and empty
placeholder AppConfig struct to establish the config/ namespace per
implementation checklist (PLAN § 14.2: core modules, then implement
config with load/save and defaults). Actual TOML schema, detection
logic, and file I/O will be implemented after storage and search layers
define their configuration requirements (path locations, tuning knobs)
to avoid premature abstraction.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (config
component), § 10 (agent auto-detection), § 11 (configuration file
layout), Cargo.toml (directories, toml, notify dependencies), AGENTS.md
line 15-26 (dotenvy pattern, config reads env not .env directly),
README.md (config/ module description).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+//! Configuration loading and management.
+//! Uses dotenvy pattern per AGENTS.md: load `.env` in main before reading env vars.
+
+pub struct AppConfig;
